design_philosophy: Research ≠ Trading. Trading Terminal = pure execution, strict minimum UI, action-focused. Zero distractions.

typography.font_family_mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace
typography.h1: text-xl md:text-2xl font-bold tracking-tight text-gray-900 uppercase

layout_and_spacing.block_style: bg-white border border-gray-200 rounded-sm shadow-sm flex flex-col overflow-hidden

password_gate.layout: fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-900/95 backdrop-blur-md
password_gate.card: bg-gray-900 p-8 border border-gray-700 rounded-sm shadow-2xl max-w-sm w-full flex flex-col gap-6
password_gate.input: w-full border-b border-gray-600 bg-transparent focus:border-white px-0 py-3 outline-none text-3xl tracking-[0.5em] text-center transition-colors font-mono text-white placeholder:text-gray-700
password_gate.button: w-full py-3 bg-white text-gray-900 font-bold uppercase tracking-widest text-sm hover:bg-gray-200 transition-colors

terminal_layout.wrapper: flex h-screen w-full bg-gray-50 overflow-hidden font-sans
terminal_layout.sidebar: h-full bg-gray-900 border-r border-gray-800 flex-shrink-0 flex flex-col

top_bar.wrapper: h-14 border-b border-gray-200 bg-white flex items-center justify-between px-6 flex-shrink-0
top_bar.title: text-sm font-bold uppercase tracking-widest flex items-center gap-2 text-gray-900
top_bar.status_indicators: flex gap-4 text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Routing: Create an isolated route for '/trading'. It must NOT be wrapped in the standard application layout (App.jsx shell). The terminal needs full screen access without standard app navigation.

2. Component - PasswordGate: Implement a password prompt overlay. In development mode (NODE_ENV === 'development'), allow bypassing or auto-fill. Use a dark theme strictly for the gate (bg-gray-900) to contrast with the terminal entry.

3. Layout - TradingTerminalLayout: Build a two-column flex layout. Left is the dark Sidebar (bg-gray-900, matching existing tech-analysis concept). Right is the light workspace (bg-gray-50) using a 100vh height and flex-col for inner scrolling.

4. Grid Framework: Use a 12-column CSS grid (bento-box style) with 'gap-4' inside the main workspace. Apply 'bg-white', 'border-gray-200', and 'rounded-sm' to all functional blocks. Maintain strict 1px flat borders with zero soft shadows.
5. Block - Top Bar: A 56px fixed header in the workspace holding the terminal title and global socket connection status.
6. Block - Chart: Allocate col-span-8 and row-span-2. Render a placeholder with grid lines or an embedded TradingView light-theme iframe.

7. Block - Decision & Why: Display the engine decision (GO_FULL, GO_REDUCED, WAIT, SKIP). Use massive typography (text-4xl or larger) for the decision string and a distinct background color based on the status. List the 'WHY' reasons as dense bullet points or minimal badges.
8. Block - Execution: Focus purely on action. Dense inputs (h-8, minimal padding) for Mode, Size Multiplier, Entry/SL/TP fields. Include a high-contrast 'EXECUTE' button.
9. Block - Microstructure: Render Imbalance, Spread, Liquidity state using horizontal progress bars or mini-sparklines. Keep data tabular and dense.
10. Block - Positions: Bottom-spanning table showing active trades and PnL. Emphasize PnL colors (Green/Red) heavily.
11. Testing: MUST attach 'data-testid' to every interactive element (Gate input, Submit button, Execution inputs, Execute trade button, Position rows).
